item.title: AI actor Tilly Norwood set to star in first feature film
item.description: AI-generated actor Tilly Norwood is set to star in her first feature film, with her creator saying that "art will be imitating life."

item.title: Trump says U.S. will win war "one way or the other" as Iran holds Khamenei's funeral
item.description: The U.S. is "going to win one way or the other," Trump said Monday, as Iran held a colossal public funeral for their slain supreme leader.

item.title: Man confesses from jail to killing wife, says he will reveal location of body
item.description: Delphine Jubillar went missing in southern France in December 2020 at the height of the COVID pandemic and her body was never found.

item.title: Key hearing begins for man accused of killing Charlie Kirk
item.description: A key hearing begins Monday for Tyler Robinson, the man accused of killing Charlie Kirk. CBS News' Carter Evans has the latest.

item.title: Beyoncé releases first new song in two years
item.description: Beyoncé released her first new song in two years, "MORNING DEW (DONK)," on the Fourth of July. Caché McClay, a music reporter for USA Today Network, has more.

item.title: FIFA criticized for lifting U.S. star's red card suspension after Trump phone call
item.description: Europe's soccer governing body and prominent commentators have criticized the decision, which allows Folarin Balogun to play against Belgium.

item.title: Russian missile and drone attack on Ukraine's capital kills at least 22
item.description: All the ballistic missiles launched by Russia struck their targets, underscoring Kyiv's need for more U.S. Patriot interceptor missiles, Ukraine officials say.

item.title: FIFA president reacts to Trump's red card phone call controversy
item.description: FIFA President Gianni Infantino reacted to the controversy after Folarin Balogun's red-card suspension was lifted, and President Trump confirmed his phone call on the matter in the Oval Office on Monday. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi has more.

item.title: White nationalist group marches in Washington during 4th of July celebrations
item.description: A white nationalist group held demonstrations in Washington, D.C., during the weekend's Fourth of July celebrations. CBS News' Nicole Sganga reports.

item.title: Here's who won — and lost — under Trump's "big, beautiful bill"
item.description: A year after President Trump signed the sweeping tax and spending package, its effects on households, businesses and federal programs are increasingly evident.

item.title: Behind the controversial decision to lift red card suspension of U.S. soccer star Folarin Balogun
item.description: Roger Bennett, founder and CEO of Men in Blazers, an independent media network that covers soccer, joins "CBS Mornings" to break down the decision to lift Folarin Balogun's red card suspension. The U.S. star is now eligible to play against Belgium on Monday.

item.title: Balogun's red card suspension lifted after Trump called FIFA chief
item.description: Balogun's reinstatement came after President Trump called FIFA President Gianni Infantino last week and asked for a review of the red card suspension.

item.title: New details on Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce's wedding, from the venue to the ceremony
item.description: After weeks of speculation, Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce officially tied the knot at Madison Square Garden in New York City. Lilia Luciano has more on the venue's transformation, guest list and the couple's vows.

item.title: 5-day preliminary hearing for Charlie Kirk's alleged killer is underway
item.description: Prosecutors will present their case this week against Tyler Robinson, the man accused of killing conservative activist Charlie Kirk.

item.title: Michigan Democrat Mallory McMorrow suspends campaign, Trump slams communism in July 4th speech
item.description: Michigan Democrat Mallory McMorrow announced Sunday that she's suspending her campaign for Senate ahead of the state's primary in early August. Meanwhile, President Trump railed against communism during his remarks in Washington, D.C., on the Fourth of July. CBS News' Fin Gomez has more.

item.title: Trump talks NATO summit, FIFA phone call during "Trump Accounts" Oval Office event
item.description: President Trump took questions from reporters during an Oval Office event launching "Trump Accounts," where he discussed the upcoming NATO summit and his phone call with FIFA President Gianni Infantino regarding U.S. striker Folarin Balogun. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi has the latest, while Lukas Weese, a staff writer for The Athletic, has more on the World Cup controversy.

item.title: Trump says he saw play that led to red card and asked FIFA president for review
item.description: President Trump said he spoke to FIFA president Gianni Infantino after seeing the play and asked for a review.

item.title: Microsoft to cut more than 3,000 jobs from ailing Xbox unit
item.description: Microsoft moves to slash costs as the video game industry faces what the tech giant calls the "most severe hardware crisis in its history."

item.title: Breaking down the World Cup red card controversy surrounding USMNT striker Folarin Balogun
item.description: Marcelo Balboa, a former member of the U.S. Men's National Team and analyst for MLS on Apple TV, joins to break down the controversy around Folarin Balogun's red card suspension.

item.title: How much interest will a $15,000 long-term CD account earn if opened this July?
item.description: Locking in today's top long-term CD rates could earn more than you'd expect over the life of the account.

item.title: "Dear David": Comfort through music, resilient painter and family helps provide accessible trips
item.description: In this week's "Beg-Knows America," CBS News contributor David Begnaud shares three inspiring stories. A woman starts an organization to bring together artists to write and perform custom songs for people facing their toughest times. Plus, a painter shows how he refuses to give up and a family helps provide accessible vacations for others.

item.title: Russia slams Ukraine's capital with deadly attack ahead of NATO summit
item.description: Russia hit Kyiv with a deadly attack ahead of Tuesday's NATO summit. CBS News' Aiden Stretch has details from Ukraine.

item.title: East Coast braces for more severe weather
item.description: The East Coast is bracing for another round of severe storms after rain, wind and lightning disrupted July Fourth celebrations. CBS News' Lana Zak has more.

item.title: Iran holds massive funeral procession for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei
item.description: Iranians attended a massive funeral procession for former Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in Tehran on Monday. CBS News' Courtney Kealy has more.

item.title: Folarin Balogun's red card suspension lifted after Trump call stirs controversy across World Cup
item.description: The soccer world is reacting after FIFA lifted Folarin Balogun's red card suspension, allowing him to play in the U.S.-Belgium game. CBS News' Nancy Cordes and Elizabeth Campbell have more.

item.title: You can now start contributing to a Trump Account. Here's what to know.
item.description: Starting July 4, people can deposit money into the new tax-deferred investment accounts, with eligible children receiving a $1,000 government contribution.

item.title: Trump holds official launch for "Trump Accounts" in the Oval Office
item.description: President Trump held an official launch for the "Trump Accounts" for children in the Oval Office on Monday, where he also rang the New York Stock Exchange bell. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi has more.

item.title: What impact could FIFA's suspension of Balogun's red card penalty have on World Cup?
item.description: Nico Cantor, an analyst for CBS Sports Golazo Network, speaks to "CBS Mornings" about FIFA's controversial decision to suspend Folarin Balogun's red card penalty and how he has traveled to all 11 host cities in the U.S. in just 30 days.

item.title: Reclaiming the lost art of listening to music
item.description: Digital music gave us the technology, and the freedom, to listen to whatever we want, whenever we want. But more and more people are going back to older, analog ways of listening. Correspondent Conor Knighton checks out the Shibuya HiFi bar in Seattle, where curated listening sessions regularly sell out. He also meets artist and engineer Devon Turnbull, whose company, Ojas, designs high-end speakers and listening rooms for public and private spaces.

item.title: Summer music highlights of 2026
item.description: Summer is the time to enjoy live music, indoors and out. Scroll through our gallery of some of 2026's leading musical acts, featuring images by CBS News photojournalist Jake Barlow and photographers Ed Spinelli and Kirstine Walton.
